name: TitleBucks Title Loans
description_short: TitleBucks provides car and motorcycle title-secured loans plus in-store personal loans across six states, with most credit types accepted and cash in as little as 30 minutes.
description_long:
TitleBucks is a title loan and pawn company headquartered in Carrollton, Texas, with physical locations across six states: Alabama, Arizona, Georgia, South Carolina, Tennessee, and Texas. The company focuses on converting vehicle titles into fast cash, serving consumers who need quick access to funds and may not qualify for traditional bank financing. It operates under the TitleBucks brand and markets itself around speed and accessibility for borrowers with imperfect credit.

TitleBucks offers three core products: car title-secured loans, motorcycle title-secured loans, and personal loans (both in-store and online). Title-secured loan amounts range from $100 to $10,000, while personal loans range from $50 to $2,500. Qualified customers in select markets may be eligible for up to $25,000. The application starts online with a short form, and in-store visits can result in cash disbursement in as little as 30 minutes. Borrowers retain use of their vehicle throughout the repayment period.

The primary distinguishing feature is accessibility — TitleBucks accepts most credit types and does not require a perfect credit history, making it an option for consumers shut out of conventional lending. The in-store process is designed for speed, with on-site vehicle appraisals and same-day funding. In Texas, the company operates as a registered Credit Service Organization and licensed Credit Access Business, meaning loans are funded through unaffiliated third-party lenders rather than TitleBucks directly — a structural distinction borrowers in that state should note.

Title loans are among the most expensive forms of consumer credit, and TitleBucks does not publish APRs or rate ranges on its public website. Borrowers who cannot repay risk losing their vehicle, which serves as collateral. The product is realistically a last resort for vehicle owners who have exhausted other options and are confident in their ability to repay quickly. Active-duty military members and their dependents are ineligible. Geographic availability is limited to six states, and state-specific rules — such as Alabama's minimum age of 19 and South Carolina's minimum loan amount of $601 — add further complexity.
pros: Cash available in as little as 30 minutes for in-store applicants
Accepts most credit types — no perfect credit history required
Borrowers keep driving their vehicle throughout the repayment period
Multiple loan types: car title, motorcycle title, and personal loans
Title loan amounts up to $10,000; up to $25,000 in select markets
Both in-store and online application options available
Personal loans available from as little as $50
cons: APR and fee schedules are not disclosed on the website — borrowers cannot comparison shop without contacting a branch
Vehicle serves as collateral; default can result in repossession
Only available in six states (AL, AZ, GA, SC, TN, TX)
Active-duty military members and their dependents are ineligible
Texas structure routes loans through third-party lenders, adding a layer of complexity to the lending relationship
best_for: Vehicle owners with poor or no credit who need emergency cash fast
Borrowers in Alabama, Arizona, Georgia, South Carolina, Tennessee, or Texas
People who need small amounts ($100–$2,500) and can repay quickly
Consumers who have been turned down by banks or credit unions and own their car outright
diagnosis: TitleBucks is best suited for vehicle owners with poor credit in its six operating states who need emergency cash within the same day and have no lower-cost alternatives available. The main caveat is that APRs on title loans are typically very high and are not disclosed upfront, and failure to repay puts the borrower's vehicle at risk.
services: Motorcycle title-secured loans (up to $2,500–$3,000 depending on state)
Loan approval for most credit types
